title: Life preservation programming
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Quote|Don't you have some kind of operating systems program that orders you to attempt to preserve the lives of intelligent beings whenever you can?"
"Yes, sir, that programming is included with all astromech droids. For a droid to deliberately harm or fail to prevent harm to a sentient being, its operating system module must be altered.|Han Solo and an R2-series astromech droid|''[[The Paradise Snare]]''}} '''Life preservation programming''', or '''LPP''', was a deeply-embedded series of commands required by law for every [[Droid/Legends|droid]] manufactured in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and later in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. LPP subroutines functioned as a final stop against behavior that would lead to harming an organic. LPP functioned independently of [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolts]] or other additional devices. They were not a 100 percent effective safeguard, but the probability of a droid going rogue was greatly lessened with enough sophisticated LPP. Manufacturing or selling a droid without life preservation programming was illegal without special government dispensation, but enforcement was lax in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. In order to pursue a career as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[4-LOM/Legends|4-LOM]], a rogue [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], required the removal of his LPP subroutines. [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] technicians eradicated this programming at the behest of the droid, and 4-LOM was integrated into Jabba's [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|criminal empire]], fully capable of harming and killing [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings.{{FigurineCite|56|Droids}}'' ==Behind the scenes== Although the subroutines are not explicitly spelled out in the source material, LPP is the rough equivalent of [[Wikipedia:Isaac Asimov|Isaac Asimov's]] [[Wikipedia:Three Laws of Robotics|Three Laws of Robotics]], the first of which prevents robots from harming or allowing, by inaction, harm to come to a human being. ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition]] *''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' *''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]'' *{{FigurineCite|56|Droids}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Droid parts]] [[Category:Software]]
